title: Re-bootstrap Keystone admin on credential rotation
description:
# GitHub Issue #381: Keystone admin credential rotation (re-bootstrap on password change)

**Description:**

This issue closes the rotation gap on the Keystone admin credential. The pieces ESO needs to deliver a rotated password to the cluster — the OpenBao KV path `kv-v2/data/bootstrap/keystone-admin`, the `ExternalSecret` definition, the controller's Secret watch, and the idempotent `keystone-manage bootstrap` invocation — are all in place. The missing step is causing the existing bootstrap `Job` to re-run after the admin Secret's `password` data byte changes; today the Job's PodSpec is byte-identical before and after rotation because the password is injected by `valueFrom.secretKeyRef`, so the SHA-256 PodSpec hash in `internal/common/job/job.go` decides the completed Job is current and never deletes it.

Concrete boundaries between what exists and what this issue adds:

1. **ESO sync from OpenBao already runs.** `deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-admin.yaml:11-29` defines an `ExternalSecret` with `refreshInterval: 1h` pulling `bootstrap/keystone-admin` (property `password`) from the `openbao-cluster-store` `ClusterSecretStore` into a Kubernetes Secret named `keystone-admin` in namespace `openstack`, `creationPolicy: Owner`. Nothing in this issue touches that file — the issue assumes ESO is the source of truth.

2. **The Secret watch already enqueues a reconcile.** `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go:752-826` defines `secretToKeystoneMapper`, registered on the controller-runtime builder at line 719 via `Watches(&corev1.Secret{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(secretToKeystoneMapper(mgr.GetClient())))`. The mapper resolves a Secret event to the referencing Keystone CRs using the `KeystoneSecretNameIndexKey` field index (line 61). The indexer's extractor (`keystoneSecretNameExtractor` at line 69) already returns both `spec.database.secretRef.name` and `spec.bootstrap.adminPasswordSecretRef.name`, so a write to the `keystone-admin` Secret already triggers reconcile for the right CRs. Nothing in this issue extends the watch.

3. **The `BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD` is injected by reference.** `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap.go:182-193` sets `BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD` via `corev1.EnvVarSource.SecretKeyRef` pointing at `keystone.Spec.Bootstrap.AdminPasswordSecretRef.Name`, key `password`. The PodSpec stores only the Secret name and key — not the data — so `json.Marshal(*corev1.PodSpec)` in `internal/common/job/job.go:34` produces identical bytes regardless of the underlying password value. The SHA-256 hash stored in the `forge.c5c3.io/pod-spec-hash` annotation (declared in `internal/common/job/job.go:28` as `PodSpecHashAnnotation`) does not change on rotation.

4. **`RunJob` already deletes-and-recreates on hash divergence.** `internal/common/job/job.go:65-91` compares `desiredHash := PodSpecHash(&job.Spec.Template.Spec)` against `existing.Annotations[PodSpecHashAnnotation]` on completed Jobs and calls `c.Delete(...)` with `DeletePropagationBackground` followed by `createJobWithHash` when they differ. This is exactly the pluggable surface to drive a re-bootstrap — the issue's task is to make the desired-hash depend on password content.

5. **The pattern to mirror lives in the apply side, not the rotate-and-stage side.** The issue's "split-compute-write" reference to `reconcile_credential.go` and `reconcile_deployment.go` is the model for Part 2 (Model B, opt-in). For Part 1, the more direct analog is `job.PodSpecHash` itself: stamp something password-derived onto `Spec.Template.Annotations` so it becomes part of the marshalled PodSpec the hash already covers. `buildKeystoneDeployment` in `reconcile_deployment.go:144-280` does not currently stamp `fernet-keys-hash` or `credential-keys-hash` annotations — the issue's reference to those names describes a hypothetical pattern, and `reconcile_deployment.go:60-62` explicitly documents that the keystone Deployment intentionally has no hash annotations because Secret data changes are picked up by kubelet projection (CC-0074). The bootstrap Job, by contrast, is a one-shot — it does not have an analogous projection path and is the correct place for a content-derived hash.

6. **The bootstrap pod template currently has no `Annotations` map.** `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap.go:163-215` constructs `batchv1.Job` with `Template: corev1.PodTemplateSpec{Spec: corev1.PodSpec{...}}` — no `ObjectMeta` on the template. Stamping an annotation requires adding `ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Annotations: map[string]string{...}}` to the `PodTemplateSpec`. The `json.Marshal` in `PodSpecHash` hashes `Spec.Template.Spec` (note: `Spec`, not the full `Template`). **Read `internal/common/job/job.go:31-37` carefully**: `PodSpecHash` takes `*corev1.PodSpec`, not `*corev1.PodTemplateSpec`. A naive stamp on `Template.ObjectMeta.Annotations` will not change the hash. The implementation must either (a) extend `PodSpecHash` / `RunJob` to hash the full `PodTemplateSpec` so pod-template annotations participate (clean and reusable across operators), or (b) inject the hash as a container-level annotation surrogate — e.g., as an additional `Env` entry on the bootstrap container whose `Value` is the hex digest of the password. Option (a) is the right primitive because it generalizes (other Jobs would benefit) and option (b) leaks the dependency to the container spec.

7. **`BootstrapReady` transitions are already wired.** `reconcile_bootstrap.go:38-75` sets `BootstrapReady=False` with reason `BootstrapInProgress` when `RunJob` returns `done=false`, `BootstrapReady=False` with reason `BootstrapFailed` on error, and `BootstrapReady=True` with reason `BootstrapComplete` on success. The aggregate `Ready` condition is computed in `keystone_controller.go:105-119` from `subConditionTypes` which already includes `"BootstrapReady"`. When the password-hash change forces a re-run, the existing condition machinery already produces the cutover that the issue's acceptance criteria require.

8. **The Secret data is available to read directly during reconcile.** The reconciler embeds `client.Client` (see `KeystoneReconciler` struct in `keystone_controller.go:122-135`); reading the admin Secret to compute a SHA-256 over `password` is a single `r.Get(ctx, client.ObjectKey{Namespace: keystone.Namespace, Name: keystone.Spec.Bootstrap.AdminPasswordSecretRef.Name}, &secret)` call inside `reconcileBootstrap`. Sequencing in `keystone_controller.go:241-244` already guarantees `reconcileSecrets` has run before `reconcileBootstrap` (line 369-373), and `reconcileSecrets:140-149` verifies the `password` key is present on the admin Secret before SecretsReady flips True — so the Get inside `reconcileBootstrap` will not race the ESO sync.

9. **Annotation key namespacing has an existing convention.** `forge.c5c3.io/` is used for cross-cutting reconciler metadata (`PodSpecHashAnnotation = "forge.c5c3.io/pod-spec-hash"` in `internal/common/job/job.go:28`, `RotationCompletedAnnotation = "forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at"` in `rotation_staging.go:34`, `dbJobUIDAnnotationFormat = "forge.c5c3.io/last-%s-job-uid"` in `reconcile_database.go:39`). The issue text proposes `keystone.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash`; this diverges from the established `forge.c5c3.io/` namespace. **Adopt the existing convention** and name the annotation `forge.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash` so it sorts alongside other forge-managed metadata. The issue's name is illustrative, not normative — its example phrase "e.g., `keystone.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash`" already signals this is a placeholder.

10. **Part 2 (Model B) explicitly stays optional and feature-gated.** The issue marks Model B as "opt-in" with a "new spec gate, e.g. `spec.bootstrap.passwordRotation.{enabled,schedule}`, default disabled". This issue commits to Model A (Part 1) only; Model B is left as a follow-up because it requires (a) a new `BootstrapSpec` field (currently `BootstrapSpec` in `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go:388-407` has only `AdminUser`, `AdminPasswordSecretRef`, `Region`, `PublicEndpoint`), (b) a new CronJob + staging Secret + RBAC split mirroring `reconcile_fernet.go:159-217`, (c) an OpenBao `PushSecret` writing the new password to OpenBao via `secrets.EnsurePushSecret` (used in `reconcile_fernet.go:137`), and (d) an OpenBao policy grant for the staging path. Each of those is a discrete change and they are explicitly out of scope of this issue per the issue body's "Model B may follow as a separate change" line.

**Motivation:**

The Keystone `admin` user is the credential of last resort for the cloud control plane: it is the account operators reach for during incident response, the account K-ORC uses for admin flows, and the account CI uses for cluster validation. A credential without a rotation path accumulates risk on every day it is unrotated, and "rotate by tearing down the cluster" is not a rotation path — it is a disaster-recovery exercise.

Most of the rotation pipeline is already in production. ESO already re-syncs `bootstrap/keystone-admin` from OpenBao to the cluster Secret; the controller already watches that Secret and enqueues a reconcile; `keystone-manage bootstrap` is already idempotent. We have built every step except the last one — applying the new password — and the cost of finishing it is small (a controller-side SHA-256 over one Secret data byte, plumbed into the existing PodSpec-hash gate that already drives Job re-runs on operator upgrade). Closing this gap removes the last excuse for operating with a static admin password, and it shrinks the blast radius of a compromise from "until cluster rebuild" to "until next ESO refresh + reconcile + bootstrap-job lifetime" — which is on the order of an hour in the default ESO configuration and minutes once an operator forces a refresh.

This work also unlocks the opt-in `passwordRotation` CronJob (Model B). That CronJob is what lets us schedule rotation rather than depend on an operator remembering to `bao kv put`; it is on the roadmap, but it can only land cleanly on top of Part 1 because the CronJob's output (a new password landing in OpenBao via `PushSecret`) reaches Keystone via exactly the same ESO → Secret → operator → Job path that Part 1 hardens.

**Affected Areas:**

- operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap.go — `reconcileBootstrap` reads the admin Secret's `password` byte and computes a SHA-256 digest; `buildBootstrapJob` accepts the digest and stamps it on the pod template (mechanism TBD per concrete boundary 6).
- internal/common/job/job.go — if approach (a) from concrete boundary 6 is chosen, `PodSpecHash` and `RunJob` are extended to hash the full `corev1.PodTemplateSpec` (not only `PodSpec`); existing call sites (`reconcile_bootstrap.go`, `reconcile_database.go`, `reconcile_policyvalidation.go`) and their unit tests update transparently because the hash they currently store still uniquely identifies the template they construct.
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap_test.go — new tests cover (i) admin Secret password change deletes the completed Job and creates a fresh one with a different hash annotation; (ii) unchanged admin Secret leaves the completed Job in place; (iii) `BootstrapReady` flips through `BootstrapInProgress` → `BootstrapComplete` on cutover; (iv) missing/empty `password` key surfaces a clean error rather than an empty-data NPE in the hashing path.
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go — envtest coverage of the full rotation cutover (write Secret, observe Job recreation, await `BootstrapReady=True` with the new hash).
- tests/e2e/keystone/admin-password-rotation/chainsaw-test.yaml and supporting 00-keystone-cr.yaml — new Chainsaw E2E suite covering rotate → re-bootstrap → auth-with-new-password (the existing `tests/e2e/keystone/fernet-rotation/chainsaw-test.yaml` is the template; the password rotation path differs in that it triggers a Deployment-affecting state change and an HTTP authentication probe before and after).
- docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md — the `reconcileBootstrap` section (currently around line 2441) gains a paragraph on the password-hash mechanism and a row in the Bootstrap Arguments table noting the new pod-template annotation.
- docs/guides/keystone-key-rotation.md (or a sibling document) — operator-facing how-to for rotating the admin password by writing to OpenBao, including the observable evidence (new Job, `BootstrapReady` transitions, post-rotation auth check). The existing key rotation guide is the structural template.
- architecture/docs/09-implementation/05-keystone-dependencies.md and architecture/docs/05-deployment/02-secret-management.md — these live in the `architecture/` git submodule (`.gitmodules` points to `github.com/C5C3/C5C3`); the "Admin Credential Rotation" subsection of the keystone-dependencies chapter and "mechanism 3" under Credential Rotation in the secret-management chapter both need to reflect that Part 1 is implemented. These changes happen in the upstream repo and the submodule pointer is bumped in this repo.
- No CRD changes for Part 1. `BootstrapSpec` in `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go:388-407` is unchanged, so `zz_generated.deepcopy.go` and the generated CRD YAMLs under `operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/` and `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/crds/` do not need regeneration.
- No ESO or OpenBao policy changes. `deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-admin.yaml` is correct for Part 1; `deploy/openbao/policies/eso-management.hcl` already grants the `kv-v2/data/bootstrap/*` path used by ESO.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] Rotating the admin password in OpenBao (`bao kv put kv-v2/bootstrap/keystone-admin password=<new>`) results in the `admin` user authenticating against `POST /v3/auth/tokens` with the new password within one ESO `refreshInterval` (1h, or sooner if an operator runs `kubectl annotate externalsecret keystone-admin force-sync=$(date +%s) --overwrite`) plus one operator reconcile cycle plus one bootstrap Job lifetime.
- [ ] An unchanged admin Secret never causes the bootstrap Job to be deleted-and-recreated. Verified by a unit test that re-runs `reconcileBootstrap` against a completed Job whose pod-template annotation already matches the hash of the current Secret's `password` byte; the test asserts the Job's `metadata.uid` and `metadata.resourceVersion` are unchanged after the reconcile.
- [ ] `BootstrapReady` follows the cutover semantics: it transitions to `False`/`BootstrapInProgress` while the new Job is running and back to `True`/`BootstrapComplete` once it completes. The aggregate `Ready` condition follows `BootstrapReady` per the existing `subConditionTypes` list.
- [ ] A pod template annotation derived from the admin password is present on the bootstrap Job. The key is `forge.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash` (matches the project's existing `forge.c5c3.io/*` annotation convention). The value is the SHA-256 hex digest of the `password` data byte of the Secret referenced by `keystone.Spec.Bootstrap.AdminPasswordSecretRef.Name`.
- [ ] `PodSpecHash` covers the new annotation. If `PodSpecHash` is extended to hash `corev1.PodTemplateSpec` (recommended), the change is non-breaking for existing callers — the unit tests in `reconcile_database_test.go` and `reconcile_policyvalidation_test.go` that pre-stamp `PodSpecHashAnnotation` continue to pass because they construct their expected hash with the same helper.
- [ ] `reconcileBootstrap` returns a clean, observable error (not an NPE or a misleading "Job created") when the admin Secret is missing the `password` key or has it as an empty byte slice. Tested in `reconcile_bootstrap_test.go`. (`reconcileSecrets:140-149` already gates this case before `reconcileBootstrap` runs, but the defense-in-depth check matches the convention used elsewhere in the controller.)
- [ ] Envtest integration coverage in `operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go` exercises a full rotation: drive the Keystone CR to Ready=True, update the `keystone-admin` Secret's `password` field, observe the bootstrap Job's `metadata.uid` change and its annotation update to the new SHA-256, and assert `BootstrapReady=True` re-stabilises.
- [ ] A new Chainsaw E2E suite under `tests/e2e/keystone/admin-password-rotation/` deploys a Keystone CR, captures a token with the initial admin password, updates the `keystone-admin` Secret directly (simulating ESO's `creationPolicy: Owner` write — Model A's "external" path), waits for re-bootstrap, and verifies (i) the old password fails with HTTP 401 against `POST /v3/auth/tokens`, (ii) the new password succeeds with HTTP 201, and (iii) `BootstrapReady` is observed transitioning through `False`/`BootstrapInProgress` during the cutover. The suite directory matches the project's one-feature-one-directory convention (39 existing directories under `tests/e2e/keystone/`).
- [ ] Reconciler reference doc (`docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md`) describes the admin-password-hash mechanism in the `reconcileBootstrap` section, including the annotation key, the hashing input (the `password` Secret data byte), and the fact that this is what drives Job re-runs on rotation. The existing "Idempotency" note (line 2488) is updated so it does not contradict the new behaviour (it currently says the Job can be re-run safely — which is still true and is precisely what this feature relies on).
- [ ] Operator-facing how-to documentation (under `docs/guides/`) walks through rotating the admin password by writing to OpenBao, with the observable evidence operators should expect (event stream, `kubectl get jobs`, `kubectl describe keystone`, `BootstrapReady` transitions, and the post-rotation `openstack token issue` smoke check).
- [ ] All changes touching `_types.go` are absent (Part 1 introduces no new spec fields), confirming no generated artifacts need regeneration.

**Non-Goals:**

- Model B (operator-scheduled rotation CronJob, `spec.bootstrap.passwordRotation`). Explicitly deferred to a follow-up issue per the upstream issue body. Part 1 does not introduce the spec gate, does not introduce a staging Secret, does not introduce a `PushSecret` to OpenBao, and does not introduce a new OpenBao policy grant. The split-compute-write CronJob pattern in `reconcile_fernet.go:159-217` and `reconcile_credential.go:174-217` is the future template for Model B.
- Revoking existing Fernet tokens on rotation. A password rotation does not invalidate already-issued tokens — they continue to validate until their natural expiry. If operators need immediate session invalidation, that is a separate operation (`openstack token revoke` per token, or a Fernet primary-key rotation via the existing CronJob) and is out of scope of this issue.
- Rotating service users (nova, neutron, cinder, etc.) or application credentials. The blast-radius analysis in the issue body explicitly notes that running services use their own service users / application credentials, not the admin password. Service-user rotation requires a different mechanism and is not part of this work.
- Changing ESO's `refreshInterval` from 1h. A more aggressive refresh would shorten the rotation window but is a deployment-wide trade-off (every ExternalSecret refreshes more often). Operators who need a faster cutover can force-sync the ExternalSecret with `kubectl annotate`; an SLA-driven refresh interval change is a separate conversation.
- Migrating the admin password Secret away from `creationPolicy: Owner`. The issue's "Design considerations" call out that ESO would overwrite a non-Owner write on the next refresh; keeping `Owner` and writing to OpenBao first is the correct invariant and this issue does not relax it.
- Adding a controller-side OpenBao client. Part 1 reads only the in-cluster Kubernetes Secret. Any operator-to-OpenBao traffic is Model B territory.
- Extending the watch to other Bootstrap-derived state. The Secret watch already covers `spec.bootstrap.adminPasswordSecretRef.name` via `keystoneSecretNameExtractor`. No additional Watches are added.
- Changing the hashing primitive. SHA-256 of the raw `password` byte is sufficient as a change detector — this is not a credential-storage hash, it is a comparator. No salting, no HMAC, no PBKDF2.

**References:**

- reconcileBootstrap / buildBootstrapJob (operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap.go:38-216)
- PodSpecHashAnnotation, PodSpecHash, RunJob delete-and-recreate-on-hash-divergence pattern (internal/common/job/job.go:24-117)
- secretToKeystoneMapper, KeystoneSecretNameIndexKey, keystoneSecretNameExtractor (operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go:752-826)
- reconcileSecrets admin-Secret readiness gate (operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets.go:60-158)
- Split-compute-write apply pattern, template for Model B (operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet.go:43-151)
- Split-compute-write sibling implementation (operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_credential.go:52-159)
- Explanation of why the Deployment intentionally does not stamp Secret content hashes — CC-0074 (operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go:60-62)
- ExternalSecret that Part 1 relies on (deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-admin.yaml)
- OpenBao policy already granting kv-v2/data/bootstrap/* (deploy/openbao/policies/eso-management.hcl)
- BootstrapSpec definition — context for Model B, no change in this issue (operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go:388-407)
- TestReconcileBootstrap_StaleJobDetection — fixture template for new hash-divergence tests (operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap_test.go:268-294)
- Chainsaw test template — rotation flow, pre/post token validation, manifest pattern (tests/e2e/keystone/fernet-rotation/chainsaw-test.yaml)
- reconcileBootstrap reference — target for documentation update (docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md:2440-2492)
- Operator-facing rotation how-to — structural template for new admin-password-rotation guide (docs/guides/keystone-key-rotation.md)
- Admin Credential Rotation subsection in keystone-dependencies (architecture/docs/09-implementation/05-keystone-dependencies.md) — lives in the architecture/ submodule
- Credential Rotation mechanism 3 in secret-management (architecture/docs/05-deployment/02-secret-management.md) — lives in the architecture/ submodule
- Kubernetes Operator Reconciliation pattern (idempotency, generation tracking) — the bootstrap re-run is idempotent because `keystone-manage bootstrap` is idempotent and the hash annotation makes the Job's desired identity deterministic
- OWASP A02 (Cryptographic Failures) and OWASP A07 (Identification and Authentication Failures) — single-credential rotation is a known weak spot; closing it removes a long-lived-credential audit finding
- Source GitHub issue C5C3/forge#381 (https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/381)

---

_Elaborated by [planwerk-review](https://github.com/planwerk/planwerk-review) with Claude Code_


**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/381

stories.title: Operator rotates the admin password by writing to OpenBao
stories.want: to rotate the Keystone admin password by writing a new value to OpenBao and have the running cluster pick it up automatically
stories.so_that: I can rotate the credential of last resort without tearing down and rebuilding the control plane
criteria: Writing `bao kv put kv-v2/bootstrap/keystone-admin password=<new>` eventually causes the `admin` user to authenticate with the new password
Forcing an ESO refresh (`kubectl annotate externalsecret keystone-admin force-sync=$(date +%s) --overwrite`) shortens the cutover from ~1h to one reconcile + one bootstrap Job lifetime
The cutover is observable via a new bootstrap Job (changed `metadata.uid`), `BootstrapReady` transitions, and Kubernetes events
A post-rotation `openstack token issue` (or `POST /v3/auth/tokens`) smoke check succeeds with the new password and fails with the old one

stories.title: Controller re-runs the bootstrap Job when the admin password changes
stories.want: the desired bootstrap Job identity to depend on the admin password content so a rotated Secret forces a re-bootstrap
stories.so_that: the existing PodSpec-hash delete-and-recreate gate in RunJob applies the new password
criteria: reconcileBootstrap reads the `password` data byte of the Secret named by `spec.bootstrap.adminPasswordSecretRef.name` and computes a SHA-256 hex digest
buildBootstrapJob stamps the digest on the pod template under annotation `forge.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash`
PodSpecHash covers the pod-template annotation so a changed digest changes the stored `forge.c5c3.io/pod-spec-hash` and RunJob deletes-and-recreates the completed Job
An unchanged admin Secret leaves the completed Job's `metadata.uid` and `metadata.resourceVersion` unchanged

stories.title: Operator observes correct readiness semantics during cutover
stories.want: `BootstrapReady` (and the aggregate `Ready`) to reflect the re-bootstrap accurately
stories.so_that: I can tell whether a rotation is in progress, complete, or failed from `kubectl describe keystone`
criteria: `BootstrapReady` flips to False/`BootstrapInProgress` while the new Job runs
`BootstrapReady` flips back to True/`BootstrapComplete` once the new Job completes
The aggregate `Ready` condition follows `BootstrapReady` per the existing `subConditionTypes` list
No spurious transitions occur when the password is unchanged

stories.title: Controller surfaces a clean error on a missing/empty password
stories.want: a clear, observable error when the admin Secret lacks a usable `password` key
stories.so_that: operators see a diagnosable condition instead of an NPE or a misleading 'bootstrap complete'
criteria: A Secret missing the `password` key yields BootstrapReady=False with a descriptive reason/message and a returned error (requeue), not a panic
A Secret with an empty `password` byte slice is treated the same way
No bootstrap Job is created or marked complete in the missing/empty case
The error path mirrors the read pattern used by reconcileSecrets (secrets.GetSecretValue / ErrKeyNotFound) so the defense-in-depth check is consistent

stories.title: Developer reuses a pod-template-aware hash primitive
stories.want: PodSpecHash/RunJob to hash the full PodTemplateSpec so pod-template annotations participate in change detection
stories.so_that: any Job (not just bootstrap) can drive a re-run from a content-derived annotation without leaking the dependency into the container spec
criteria: PodSpecHash hashes the full `corev1.PodTemplateSpec` (metadata + spec), not only `corev1.PodSpec`
RunJob and createJobWithHash compute the stored hash over `&job.Spec.Template`
All existing callers (reconcile_database, reconcile_policyvalidation, bootstrap) and their tests remain green after a mechanical update to the same helper
A unit test proves that changing only a pod-template annotation changes the hash

stories.title: Operator reads how to rotate the admin password
stories.want: a how-to guide and an updated reconciler reference
stories.so_that: I can perform and verify an admin-password rotation with documented, observable evidence
criteria: docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md documents the `forge.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash` annotation, its hashing input, and that it drives Job re-runs on rotation
The existing Idempotency note is updated so it does not contradict the new re-run behaviour
A new how-to under docs/guides/ walks through writing to OpenBao, force-syncing ESO, and verifying via events/jobs/conditions/token check
The how-to mirrors the structure of docs/guides/keystone-key-rotation.md (frontmatter, Background, numbered steps, Related reference)

stories.title: Security auditor confirms the rotation closes the long-lived-credential finding
stories.want: evidence that the admin password can be rotated in-place and that rotation does not weaken isolation
stories.so_that: the OWASP A02/A07 long-lived-credential audit finding can be closed
criteria: Rotation reads only the in-cluster Kubernetes Secret (no controller-side OpenBao client is added)
The hashing input is the raw `password` byte (a change comparator), with no new credential at rest beyond the existing Secret
Rotation does not modify the Keystone Deployment or roll API pods (the change is isolated to the one-shot bootstrap Job)
No new CRD field, RBAC grant, or OpenBao policy is introduced by Part 1

requirements.description: The job primitive SHALL hash the full corev1.PodTemplateSpec (metadata + spec) so pod-template annotations participate in the forge.c5c3.io/pod-spec-hash change detector.
requirements.rationale: The bootstrap PodSpec is byte-identical before/after rotation because the password is injected by secretKeyRef; only a pod-template annotation can carry the password-derived signal, and PodSpecHash currently hashes *corev1.PodSpec (job.go:34), excluding Template.ObjectMeta.

scenarios.name: Template annotation change alters the hash
scenarios.when: two Jobs are identical except for one Template.ObjectMeta.Annotations entry
scenarios.then: PodSpecHash returns different digests for them
and_then: RunJob treats the completed Job as stale and deletes-and-recreates it

scenarios.name: Existing callers remain behaviorally correct
scenarios.when: reconcile_database / reconcile_policyvalidation / bootstrap construct their Jobs unchanged (no Template.ObjectMeta set)
scenarios.then: RunJob still uniquely identifies each template and recreates only on real spec changes
and_then: a one-time recreation may occur on first reconcile after upgrade because the hash bytes changed shape; this is safe for idempotent Jobs

scenarios.name: Non-breaking for existing unit tests after mechanical update
scenarios.when: test helpers compute their expected hash with the same exported helper over the full template (&desired.Spec.Template)
scenarios.then: their pre-stamped forge.c5c3.io/pod-spec-hash matches RunJob's computed hash
and_then: completed-Job retention assertions continue to pass

requirements.description: reconcileBootstrap SHALL read the `password` data byte of the Secret named by keystone.Spec.Bootstrap.AdminPasswordSecretRef.Name, compute its SHA-256 hex digest, and buildBootstrapJob SHALL stamp that digest on the pod template under annotation `forge.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash`.
requirements.rationale: Makes the desired bootstrap-Job identity depend on password content, plumbing into the existing RunJob hash gate. The annotation key follows the established forge.c5c3.io/* convention (the issue's keystone.c5c3.io/* name is illustrative).

scenarios.name: Digest is the SHA-256 of the password byte
scenarios.when: the admin Secret's password value is V
scenarios.then: the annotation value equals hex(sha256(V))
and_then: the value is lowercase hex, 64 chars

scenarios.name: Annotation present on the bootstrap Job pod template
scenarios.when: buildBootstrapJob is invoked with the computed digest
scenarios.then: Job.Spec.Template.ObjectMeta.Annotations["forge.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash"] is set to the digest
and_then: the digest participates in PodSpecHash per REQ-001

scenarios.name: Read mirrors the reconcileSecrets pattern
scenarios.when: reconcileBootstrap reads the Secret
scenarios.then: it uses client.ObjectKey{Namespace: keystone.Namespace, Name: keystone.Spec.Bootstrap.AdminPasswordSecretRef.Name} and the literal key "password" (matching reconcile_secrets.go:136 and reconcile_bootstrap.go:190)
and_then: reconcileSecrets having gated SecretsReady=True before reconcileBootstrap (controller order 241 then 369) means the read does not race ESO sync

requirements.description: A change to the admin Secret's `password` SHALL cause the completed bootstrap Job to be deleted-and-recreated; an unchanged admin Secret SHALL NOT.
requirements.rationale: This is the rotation cutover itself and the no-churn invariant. The deletion uses the existing metav1.DeletePropagationBackground path (job.go:82).

scenarios.name: Rotation deletes and recreates
scenarios.when: a completed bootstrap Job exists with annotation hash for password A and reconcileBootstrap runs against a Secret holding password B
scenarios.then: RunJob deletes the old Job and creates a new one whose pod-template carries hash(B)
and_then: the new Job's stored forge.c5c3.io/pod-spec-hash differs from the old one
the new Job has a different metadata.uid

scenarios.name: Unchanged Secret is a no-op
scenarios.when: the completed Job's pod-template hash already matches hash of the current Secret's password
scenarios.then: RunJob leaves the Job in place
and_then: the Job's metadata.uid and metadata.resourceVersion are unchanged after reconcile

scenarios.name: Recreation is idempotent
scenarios.when: keystone-manage bootstrap runs again against an already-bootstrapped Keystone with a new password
scenarios.then: it updates the admin password without error
and_then: no duplicate admin user/project/role is created

requirements.description: BootstrapReady SHALL follow cutover semantics: False/BootstrapInProgress while the recreated Job runs and True/BootstrapComplete once it completes; the aggregate Ready SHALL follow via subConditionTypes.
requirements.rationale: The condition machinery already exists (reconcile_bootstrap.go:44-72; subConditionTypes includes BootstrapReady at keystone_controller.go:117); the feature relies on it producing the cutover.

scenarios.name: In-progress during cutover
scenarios.when: RunJob returns done=false after recreating the Job
scenarios.then: BootstrapReady is set False with reason BootstrapInProgress and the result requeues (RequeueBootstrapWait)
and_then: aggregate Ready becomes False

scenarios.name: Complete after cutover
scenarios.when: the recreated Job reaches JobComplete and RunJob returns done=true
scenarios.then: BootstrapReady is set True with reason BootstrapComplete
and_then: aggregate Ready returns to True once all sub-conditions are True

scenarios.name: Failure surfaces BootstrapFailed
scenarios.when: RunJob returns ErrJobFailed for the recreated Job
scenarios.then: BootstrapReady is set False with reason BootstrapFailed and the error message
and_then: aggregate Ready stays False

requirements.description: reconcileBootstrap SHALL return a clean, observable error (no panic, no misleading 'Job created/complete') when the admin Secret is missing the `password` key or has it as an empty byte slice.
requirements.rationale: Defense-in-depth matching the controller convention even though reconcileSecrets gates this case (reconcile_secrets.go:135-149). A nil/empty data byte would otherwise hash an empty string and silently stamp a meaningless digest.

scenarios.name: Missing password key
scenarios.when: the admin Secret has no `password` key
scenarios.then: reconcileBootstrap sets BootstrapReady=False with a descriptive reason/message and returns an error (requeue)
and_then: no bootstrap Job is created and no BootstrapComplete is set

scenarios.name: Empty password value
scenarios.when: the admin Secret has `password` set to an empty byte slice
scenarios.then: reconcileBootstrap treats it as invalid with the same clean error
and_then: the hashing path is never reached with empty input

scenarios.name: Secret entirely absent
scenarios.when: the named admin Secret does not exist
scenarios.then: secrets.GetSecretValue surfaces a wrapped error which is reported on BootstrapReady
and_then: the reconcile requeues rather than panicking

requirements.description: Part 1 SHALL introduce no changes to *_types.go and therefore no regenerated artifacts (deepcopy, CRD bases, Helm CRDs).
requirements.rationale: BootstrapSpec (keystone_types.go:388-407) is unchanged; Model B (the passwordRotation spec gate) is explicitly deferred.

scenarios.name: No API surface change
scenarios.when: the diff is reviewed
scenarios.then: no file under operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/ is modified
and_then: zz_generated.deepcopy.go, config/crd/bases/, and helm/keystone-operator/crds/ are untouched

scenarios.name: make manifests/generate is a no-op
scenarios.when: controller-gen is run after the change
scenarios.then: it produces no diff
and_then: CRD drift checks pass

requirements.description: The reconciler reference doc SHALL document the admin-password-hash mechanism and the Idempotency note SHALL be updated so it does not contradict the re-run behaviour.
requirements.rationale: docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md reconcileBootstrap section (lines ~2441-2492) must reflect the new behaviour; the Idempotency note (~2488) is exactly what makes the re-run safe.

scenarios.name: Annotation documented
scenarios.when: an operator reads the reconcileBootstrap reference section
scenarios.then: they find the annotation key forge.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash, its hashing input (the `password` Secret data byte), and that it drives Job re-runs on rotation
and_then: the documentation references the forge.c5c3.io/pod-spec-hash gate

scenarios.name: Idempotency note consistent
scenarios.when: the Idempotency note is read after the change
scenarios.then: it still states keystone-manage bootstrap is idempotent and adds that this is what makes password-triggered re-runs safe
and_then: there is no contradiction with the new delete-and-recreate behaviour

requirements.description: An operator-facing how-to guide under docs/guides/ SHALL walk through rotating the admin password by writing to OpenBao, with the observable evidence operators should expect.
requirements.rationale: User-facing operation requires a Diataxis how-to; docs/guides/keystone-key-rotation.md is the structural template.

scenarios.name: Procedure is executable
scenarios.when: an operator follows the guide
scenarios.then: they run `bao kv put kv-v2/bootstrap/keystone-admin password=<new>`, optionally force-sync ESO, and observe re-bootstrap
and_then: the guide lists kubectl get jobs, kubectl describe keystone, the BootstrapReady transitions, and an openstack token issue smoke check

scenarios.name: Structure mirrors the key-rotation guide
scenarios.when: the guide is compared to keystone-key-rotation.md
scenarios.then: it has frontmatter (quadrant: operator), a Background section, numbered steps, and a Related reference section
and_then: it links to the reconciler reference and the new Chainsaw suite

requirements.description: A new Chainsaw E2E suite under tests/e2e/keystone/admin-password-rotation/ SHALL prove old-password 401, new-password 201, and BootstrapReady transitions across a rotation.
requirements.rationale: End-to-end proof against a live API; tests/e2e/keystone/fernet-rotation is the template. Part 1's external path is simulated by writing the keystone-admin Secret directly (ESO creationPolicy: Owner).

scenarios.name: Old password rejected after rotation
scenarios.when: the keystone-admin Secret password is updated and re-bootstrap completes
scenarios.then: POST /v3/auth/tokens with the original password returns HTTP 401
and_then: the probe runs via kubectl exec python3 urllib (no curl in the image)

scenarios.name: New password accepted after rotation
scenarios.when: re-bootstrap completes
scenarios.then: POST /v3/auth/tokens with the new password returns HTTP 201 and an X-Subject-Token header
and_then: a token captured with the initial password before rotation also returned 201

scenarios.name: BootstrapReady transitions observed
scenarios.when: the rotation is in progress
scenarios.then: the suite observes BootstrapReady False/BootstrapInProgress during cutover and True/BootstrapComplete afterward
and_then: the Keystone CR returns to Ready=True/AllReady

requirements.description: Envtest integration coverage in integration_test.go SHALL exercise a full rotation cutover: drive to Ready=True, update the keystone-admin Secret password, observe the bootstrap Job uid change and annotation update, and assert BootstrapReady=True re-stabilises.
requirements.rationale: Integration tier exists (envtest harness) and the issue requires it; uses the established driveFullReconciliation + simulators helpers.

scenarios.name: Cutover drives a new Job
scenarios.when: the keystone-admin Secret password is updated after Ready=True
scenarios.then: the bootstrap Job's metadata.uid changes and its forge.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash annotation equals the new SHA-256
and_then: BootstrapReady passes through False/BootstrapInProgress

scenarios.name: Re-stabilisation
scenarios.when: the recreated Job is simulated complete
scenarios.then: BootstrapReady returns to True/BootstrapComplete and aggregate Ready returns to True
and_then: a subsequent reconcile with the same password is a no-op (uid stable)

requirements.description: The implementation SHOULD use SHA-256 of the raw `password` byte as a pure change comparator (no salt/HMAC/PBKDF2) and SHOULD adopt the forge.c5c3.io/* annotation namespace.
requirements.rationale: This is a change detector, not credential storage; the established namespace (PodSpecHashAnnotation, RotationCompletedAnnotation, dbJobUIDAnnotationFormat) is forge.c5c3.io/*.

scenarios.name: Hashing primitive is unsalted SHA-256
scenarios.when: the digest is computed
scenarios.then: it is sha256.Sum256([]byte(password)) hex-encoded, with no salt or keying
and_then: the same password always yields the same digest

scenarios.name: Namespace convention
scenarios.when: the annotation key is chosen
scenarios.then: it is forge.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash
and_then: it sorts alongside other forge-managed metadata

requirements.description: Rotation SHALL be isolated to the bootstrap Job: it SHALL NOT modify the Keystone Deployment, SHALL NOT roll API pods, and SHALL NOT add a controller-side OpenBao client.
requirements.rationale: The Deployment intentionally has no content-hash annotations (CC-0074, reconcile_deployment.go:60-62); Part 1 reads only the in-cluster Secret. Model B (PushSecret/OpenBao traffic) is out of scope.

scenarios.name: No Deployment rollout
scenarios.when: the admin password rotates
scenarios.then: the Keystone Deployment template hash is unchanged and API pods are not restarted
and_then: the E2E suite verifies pod-template-hash pod UIDs are stable across rotation

scenarios.name: No OpenBao client added
scenarios.when: the controller code is reviewed
scenarios.then: no new OpenBao/bao client import or call is introduced in the keystone controller
and_then: only the in-cluster Kubernetes Secret is read

tasks.title: Extend PodSpecHash/RunJob to hash the full corev1.PodTemplateSpec (REQ-001). In internal/common/job/job.go change PodSpecHash to take *corev1.PodTemplateSpec and json.Marshal it (was *corev1.PodSpec at line 34); update the two internal computations in RunJob (line ~72) and createJobWithHash (line ~108) to PodSpecHash(&job.Spec.Template). Keep PodSpecHashAnnotation and DeletePropagationBackground unchanged. Leave a # DECISION/comment noting approach (a) was chosen over a container-env surrogate because it generalises and does not leak into the container spec.

Add job primitive tests (REQ-001) in internal/common/job/job_test.go: TestPodSpecHash_TemplateAnnotationParticipates (annotation changes the digest), TestRunJob_RecreatesOnTemplateAnnotationChange, and TestRunJob_NoRecreateWhenTemplateUnchanged. Update existing helpers (testCompletedJobWithHash, hash assertions ~lines 68-87/133/229) to compute expected hash over the full template (&job.Spec.Template).

Update existing Job-hash test helpers for the signature change (REQ-001) so the suite stays green. In reconcile_database_test.go (completedDBSyncJob:116, failedDBSyncJob:134, completedSchemaCheckJob:159, completedUpgradeJob:1058) and reconcile_policyvalidation_test.go (completedPolicyValidationJob:97, failedPolicyValidationJob:112, runningPolicyValidationJob:126, StaleJob_DeletedAndRecreated:432) change job.PodSpecHash(&desired.Spec.Template.Spec) to job.PodSpecHash(&desired.Spec.Template). No production change to reconcile_database.go / reconcile_policyvalidation.go (they build Jobs without Template.ObjectMeta).

Stamp the admin-password-hash annotation in buildBootstrapJob (REQ-002, REQ-011). In reconcile_bootstrap.go add const adminPasswordHashAnnotation = "forge.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash"; add an adminPasswordHash string parameter to buildBootstrapJob (currently buildBootstrapJob(keystone, configMapName, fernetSecretName)); set Template.ObjectMeta = metav1.ObjectMeta{Annotations: map[string]string{adminPasswordHashAnnotation: adminPasswordHash}} (the PodTemplateSpec currently has no ObjectMeta).

Read the admin Secret and compute the digest in reconcileBootstrap (REQ-002, REQ-005). Add imports crypto/sha256 and encoding/hex. Build adminSecretKey = client.ObjectKey{Namespace: keystone.Namespace, Name: keystone.Spec.Bootstrap.AdminPasswordSecretRef.Name}; read password via secrets.GetSecretValue(ctx, r.Client, adminSecretKey, "password"); on error or empty value set BootstrapReady=False (recommended reason AdminSecretInvalid) with a descriptive message, emit a Warning event, and return the error (requeue) before any Job is built. Compute digest := hex.EncodeToString(sha256.Sum256([]byte(password))[:]) and pass it to buildBootstrapJob.

Unit-test the annotation stamping and digest (REQ-002) in reconcile_bootstrap_test.go: TestBuildBootstrapJob_AdminPasswordHashAnnotation asserts Template.ObjectMeta.Annotations["forge.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash"] == hex(sha256(password)). Update the existing bootstrap Job helpers (completedBootstrapJob/failedBootstrapJob/runningBootstrapJob) to compute the pre-stamped pod-spec-hash over &desired.Spec.Template and to set the admin-password-hash annotation consistent with buildBootstrapJob.

Unit-test rotation recreation and no-churn (REQ-003, REQ-004) in reconcile_bootstrap_test.go: TestReconcileBootstrap_PasswordChangeRecreatesJob (old Job stamped hash(A), Secret holds B -> old deleted, new Job carries hash(B), different pod-spec-hash), TestReconcileBootstrap_UnchangedPasswordRetainsJob (uid/resourceVersion stable), and TestReconcileBootstrap_CutoverConditionTransitions (False/BootstrapInProgress then True/BootstrapComplete). Mirror TestReconcileBootstrap_StaleJobDetection (lines 268-294) and the newBootstrapTestReconciler/bootstrapKeystone fixtures.

Unit-test the missing/empty password error paths (REQ-005) in reconcile_bootstrap_test.go: TestReconcileBootstrap_MissingPasswordKeyCleanError and TestReconcileBootstrap_EmptyPasswordCleanError. Assert BootstrapReady=False with the chosen reason, an error returned, no Job created, and no panic (admin Secret built with WithObjects but missing/empty `password`).

Add envtest rotation cutover coverage (REQ-010) in integration_test.go: TestIntegration_AdminPasswordRotationCutover. Use setupEnvTestWithController + driveFullReconciliation to reach Ready=True; capture the bootstrap Job uid; update the keystone-admin Secret `password` (client Update); Eventually assert the bootstrap Job uid changes and its forge.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash annotation equals the new SHA-256; simulators.SimulateJobComplete the new Job; waitForCondition BootstrapReady=True and Ready=True. Follow SkipIfEnvTestUnavailable + direct-client + GenerateName-namespace conventions (CC-0012).

Add envtest no-churn coverage (REQ-003) in integration_test.go: TestIntegration_AdminPasswordUnchangedNoChurn. After Ready=True, re-trigger reconcile without changing the Secret and Consistently assert the bootstrap Job uid is stable and Ready stays True.

Create the Chainsaw E2E CR manifest (REQ-009): tests/e2e/keystone/admin-password-rotation/00-keystone-cr.yaml. Base it on tests/e2e/keystone/fernet-rotation/00-keystone-cr.yaml with a distinct CR name (e.g. keystone-adminpw) to avoid namespace collisions in the shared `openstack` namespace; ensure spec.bootstrap.adminPasswordSecretRef points at the keystone-admin Secret.

Create the Chainsaw E2E test (REQ-009, REQ-004, REQ-012): tests/e2e/keystone/admin-password-rotation/chainsaw-test.yaml. Steps: apply 00-keystone-cr.yaml; assert Ready=True/AllReady; capture API pod-template-hash pod UIDs; capture a token with the initial password via kubectl exec python3 urllib POST /v3/auth/tokens (expect 201, grab X-Subject-Token); update the keystone-admin Secret `password` directly (kubectl patch/apply, base64) to simulate ESO Owner write; poll for re-bootstrap (new bootstrap Job + BootstrapReady cutover); assert old password -> 401 and new password -> 201; assert pod UIDs unchanged (no Deployment rollout). Include catch blocks dumping pod/job logs, describe, and events per the fernet-rotation template.

Wire the new E2E suite into the existing runner (REQ-009): confirm tests/e2e/keystone/admin-password-rotation/ is auto-discovered by the existing Chainsaw CI workflow; if the workflow enumerates suite directories explicitly, add the new directory to that list. Do not create a new CI pipeline (existing Go + e2e CI already covers unit/integration/e2e tiers).

Update the reconciler reference (REQ-007) in docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md reconcileBootstrap section (~2441-2492): add a paragraph describing forge.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash (key, hashing input = the `password` Secret data byte, and that it drives Job re-runs via the forge.c5c3.io/pod-spec-hash gate); add a note/row recording the new pod-template annotation; update the Idempotency note (~2488) so it states the idempotent bootstrap is what makes password-triggered re-runs safe (no contradiction). Reference CC-0108.

Write the operator how-to (REQ-008): new docs/guides/keystone-admin-password-rotation.md mirroring docs/guides/keystone-key-rotation.md (frontmatter quadrant: operator; Background; numbered steps; Related reference). Steps: write the new password to OpenBao (`bao kv put kv-v2/bootstrap/keystone-admin password=<new>`), optionally force-sync ESO (`kubectl annotate externalsecret keystone-admin force-sync=$(date +%s) --overwrite`), observe the new bootstrap Job (`kubectl get jobs`), the BootstrapReady transitions (`kubectl describe keystone`), the event stream, and a post-rotation `openstack token issue` smoke check. Link to the reconciler reference and the new Chainsaw suite. Reference CC-0108.

Confirm no generated artifacts changed (REQ-006): verify no operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/*_types.go change, and that controller-gen (make manifests/generate or equivalent) produces no diff to zz_generated.deepcopy.go, config/crd/bases/, or helm/keystone-operator/crds/. This is a verification step, not a code change; record the result.

test_specifications.story: Developer reuses a pod-template-aware hash primitive
test_specifications.expected: Two PodTemplateSpecs identical except for one ObjectMeta.Annotations entry produce different PodSpecHash digests (proves the hash now covers Template metadata)

test_specifications.story: Developer reuses a pod-template-aware hash primitive
test_specifications.expected: A completed Job whose stored forge.c5c3.io/pod-spec-hash predates a pod-template annotation change is deleted (Background propagation) and recreated with the new hash

test_specifications.story: Controller re-runs the bootstrap Job when the admin password changes
test_specifications.expected: After updating helpers to hash the full template, an unchanged completed Job is retained (no delete/create)

test_specifications.story: Controller re-runs the bootstrap Job when the admin password changes
test_specifications.expected: buildBootstrapJob stamps Template.ObjectMeta.Annotations["forge.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash"] equal to the hex(sha256(password)) passed in

test_specifications.story: Controller re-runs the bootstrap Job when the admin password changes
test_specifications.expected: Given a completed Job stamped with hash(passwordA) and an admin Secret holding passwordB, reconcile deletes the old Job and creates a new one whose annotation is hash(passwordB); the new Job's pod-spec-hash differs

test_specifications.story: Operator observes correct readiness semantics during cutover
test_specifications.expected: Given a completed Job stamped with hash of the current Secret password, reconcile leaves the Job's metadata.uid and metadata.resourceVersion unchanged

test_specifications.story: Operator observes correct readiness semantics during cutover

test_specifications.story: Controller surfaces a clean error on a missing/empty password
test_specifications.expected: An admin Secret without a `password` key yields BootstrapReady=False with a descriptive reason and a returned error; no Job is created; no panic

test_specifications.story: Controller surfaces a clean error on a missing/empty password

test_specifications.story: Developer reuses a pod-template-aware hash primitive
test_specifications.expected: Existing DB-job helpers updated to compute the expected hash over &desired.Spec.Template (not &...Spec) continue to pass their retention/recreation assertions

test_specifications.story: Developer reuses a pod-template-aware hash primitive

test_specifications.story: Operator rotates the admin password by writing to OpenBao

test_specifications.story: Operator observes correct readiness semantics during cutover
test_specifications.expected: Re-reconciling a Ready Keystone without changing the admin Secret leaves the bootstrap Job metadata.uid unchanged and Ready stays True (no spurious cutover)

test_specifications.story: Operator rotates the admin password by writing to OpenBao
test_specifications.expected: Deploy Keystone CR; wait Ready=True/AllReady; capture a token with the initial password (HTTP 201); update the keystone-admin Secret `password` directly; wait for re-bootstrap; assert old password -> HTTP 401 and new password -> HTTP 201 against POST /v3/auth/tokens; observe BootstrapReady False/BootstrapInProgress during cutover; verify API pod-template-hash pod UIDs are stable (no rollout)

test_specifications.story: Operator observes correct readiness semantics during cutover
test_specifications.expected: The suite asserts the Keystone CR shows BootstrapReady transitioning to False/BootstrapInProgress during the cutover and back to True/BootstrapComplete afterward

review_criteria: [blocker] PodSpecHash hashes the full corev1.PodTemplateSpec and RunJob/createJobWithHash compute over &job.Spec.Template; the bootstrap pod-template carries forge.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash = hex(sha256(password)), and a password change deletes-and-recreates the completed Job (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003).
[blocker] An unchanged admin Secret never recreates the bootstrap Job: a unit/integration test asserts metadata.uid and metadata.resourceVersion are unchanged after reconcile (REQ-003).
[blocker] Missing or empty `password` produces BootstrapReady=False with a clear reason and a returned error, no Job creation, and no panic — verified by dedicated tests (REQ-005).

[critical] All existing Job-hash test helpers in job_test.go, reconcile_database_test.go, and reconcile_policyvalidation_test.go are updated to the full-template helper input and the whole Go suite is green; no production logic in reconcile_database.go/reconcile_policyvalidation.go changed (REQ-001).

[critical] BootstrapReady follows cutover semantics (False/BootstrapInProgress -> True/BootstrapComplete) and aggregate Ready follows via subConditionTypes, verified in unit + integration tiers (REQ-004, REQ-010).

[critical] The Chainsaw E2E suite proves old-password 401 and new-password 201 against POST /v3/auth/tokens, observes the BootstrapReady transition, and confirms no Deployment rollout (stable pod UIDs); it is discovered by the existing e2e CI (REQ-009, REQ-012).
[critical] No file under operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/ is modified and controller-gen produces no diff (REQ-006); no controller-side OpenBao client is added (REQ-012).

[minor] Reference doc documents the annotation/mechanism and updates the Idempotency note without contradiction; a new how-to under docs/guides/ mirrors the key-rotation guide structure and links back to the reference and the new suite (REQ-007, REQ-008).

[minor] Annotation uses the forge.c5c3.io/* namespace and the digest is unsalted SHA-256 of the raw password byte (a comparator, not a storage hash) (REQ-011); no files inside the architecture/ submodule are modified in this worktree.
implementation_notes:
ARCHITECTURE (Part 1 / Model A only):

The rotation pipeline already exists end-to-end except the apply step. This feature makes the desired bootstrap-Job identity depend on the admin password content, so the existing RunJob hash gate (internal/common/job/job.go) performs the cutover.

Data flow (rotation):
  OpenBao kv-v2/bootstrap/keystone-admin
      | ESO ExternalSecret (refreshInterval 1h, creationPolicy: Owner)  [unchanged]
      v
  Secret openstack/keystone-admin (data.password)
      | Secret watch -> secretToKeystoneMapper (KeystoneSecretNameIndexKey)  [unchanged]
      v
  Reconcile -> reconcileSecrets (gates SecretsReady on password key)  [unchanged, runs first]
      v
  reconcileBootstrap:
     read password via secrets.GetSecretValue(adminSecretKey, "password")  [NEW]
     digest = hex(sha256(password))                                        [NEW]
     buildBootstrapJob(..., digest) stamps Template.ObjectMeta.Annotations[forge.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash]  [NEW]
      v
  job.RunJob: desiredHash = PodSpecHash(&job.Spec.Template)  [CHANGED: was &job.Spec.Template.Spec]
     if desiredHash != existing[forge.c5c3.io/pod-spec-hash] on a completed Job -> Delete(Background) + recreate  [unchanged mechanism]
      v
  keystone-manage bootstrap re-runs idempotently with the new password; BootstrapReady cutover -> aggregate Ready.

KEY DECISION (concrete boundary 6): approach (a) — extend PodSpecHash/RunJob to hash the full PodTemplateSpec — is chosen over approach (b) — a container-level env surrogate. (a) generalises to any Job and keeps the dependency out of the container spec. The trade-off: PodSpecHash's signature changes from *corev1.PodSpec to *corev1.PodTemplateSpec, so the THREE existing test files that pre-stamp forge.c5c3.io/pod-spec-hash via job.PodSpecHash(&desired.Spec.Template.Spec) must mechanically switch to &desired.Spec.Template (job_test.go, reconcile_database_test.go, reconcile_policyvalidation_test.go, and the bootstrap helpers). This is the only meaning under which the acceptance criterion 'non-breaking for existing callers' holds — they pass because they use the SAME helper over the SAME object as RunJob, not because zero edits are needed. On the first reconcile after upgrade, every completed Job whose stored hash was computed over PodSpec will be seen as stale once and recreated; this is safe because db/policy/bootstrap Jobs are idempotent.

READ SAFETY: reconcileSecrets (controller order line 241) runs before reconcileBootstrap (line 369) and flips SecretsReady only after verifying the `password` key (reconcile_secrets.go:135-149), so the GetSecretValue read does not race the ESO sync. The missing/empty-password check in reconcileBootstrap is defense-in-depth matching the controller convention; reuse secrets.GetSecretValue (wraps ErrKeyNotFound) and additionally guard the empty-string case.

CONDITION REASON: a dedicated reason (recommended: AdminSecretInvalid) for the missing/empty path is clearer than reusing BootstrapFailed; either is acceptable. Condition REASONS do not need registration (only condition TYPES appear in subConditionTypes / subReconcilerConditionTypes), so no metrics/coverage change is required.

PASSWORD KEY CONSTANT: the literal "password" is used in both reconcile_secrets.go:136 and reconcile_bootstrap.go:190; keep the literal to match the existing pattern (a shared const is a reasonable but out-of-scope DRY cleanup — mention, do not act).

SUBMODULE CONSTRAINT (critical): architecture/docs/09-implementation/05-keystone-dependencies.md and architecture/docs/05-deployment/02-secret-management.md live in the architecture/ git submodule (github.com/C5C3/C5C3) and are READ-ONLY from this worktree. Do NOT edit them here. They are updated upstream and the submodule pointer is bumped in a separate change (see deferred_items). Only docs/ (this repo) is edited in Part 1.

TEST TIERS: unit (controller + job package _test.go), integration (envtest integration_test.go via testutil.SetupKeystoneEnvTestWithController, Gomega Eventually/Consistently, direct non-caching client per CC-0012, GenerateName namespaces, simulators.SimulateJobComplete), and e2e (Chainsaw; HTTP probes via kubectl exec python3 urllib since the image has no curl; POST /v3/auth/tokens returns 201 on success / 401 on bad password; use pod-template-hash selector to exclude job pods and detect rollouts). All three tiers are covered.

NON-FUNCTIONAL: the rotation window is one ESO refreshInterval (1h, or immediate on force-sync) + one reconcile + one bootstrap Job lifetime. No Deployment rollout (CC-0074: kubelet projection handles in-place Secret data for the Deployment; the one-shot Job has no such path, which is exactly why the content hash belongs on the Job).
